Todo.title: Todo
Todo.description: Represents a single to-do item managed by a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Todo entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this to-do item, corresponding to the Firebase Authentication UID. (Relationship: User 1:N Todo)

title.description: The main text or short description of the to-do item.

description.description: An optional, more detailed description of the to-do item, which can also store additional notes or AI-generated sub-tasks.

completed.description: Indicates whether the to-do item has been marked as complete.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the to-do item was created. Used for ordering by newest first.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the to-do item was last updated.

definition.description: Stores individual to-do items, each owned by a specific user. The 'userId' field within the Todo document explicitly denormalizes ownership for authorization independence, complementing the path-based authorization. All documents in this collection share the same security posture: only the authenticated user matching the '{userId}' path segment and the 'userId' document field can read or write.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this collection of to-do items, corresponding to the Firebase Authentication UID.

params.name: todoId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific to-do item within the user's collection.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to be secure, scalable, and highly debuggable, primarily by enforcing strong authorization independence and clear ownership. Each user's to-do items are stored in a dedicated subcollection under their unique user ID: `/users/{userId}/todos/{todoId}`. This design achieves **Authorization Independence** by ensuring that the `userId` from the path (`{userId}`) explicitly matches `request.auth.uid` and also that the `userId` field denormalized within the `Todo` document (`resource.data.userId`) also matches `request.auth.uid`. This triple-layered check (path, document field, and auth token) eliminates the need for any `get()` operations in security rules, allowing for atomic writes and simplified rule logic. The use of a dedicated `userId` field within each `Todo` document is a direct application of the denormalization mandate. 

This structure also directly supports **Query-Aware Security Rules (QAPs)**. When a client requests data from `/users/{request.auth.uid}/todos`, all documents within that collection inherently belong to the authenticated user. This means that the security rules do not need to act as filters to remove unauthorized documents from a larger result set. Instead, the path itself enforces the access policy, guaranteeing that any `list` operation on this path will only return documents that the authenticated user is authorized to see. This 'structural segregation' ensures a homogeneous security posture for all documents within a given user's `todos` subcollection, making rules straightforward and efficient.
